commonIssues:
Frequent crashing of the online Civil Registration System (CRS) portal (crstn.org) and e-Sevai centres makes it difficult to initiate or complete applications.

How to fix:

  • Try the CRS Tamil Nadu Portal during early morning hours (6–9 AM) when traffic is lower.
  • Visit your nearest e-Sevai Centre — operators have direct backend access that may bypass public portal issues.
  • If the portal is down, submit Form No. 2 offline at the local Municipal/Panchayat office and request a stamped acknowledgement.
  • Helpline: 1800-102-5553 (TN e-Services)

Registration after 30 days to one year requires a late fee and special permission. Registration after one year requires orders from a First Class Judicial Magistrate/RDO.

How to fix:

  • Within 30 days: Register at local municipal office — free of charge.
  • 30 days to 1 year: Pay late fee and get permission from the Sub-Registrar / Health Officer of the local ULB.
  • After 1 year: Obtain an order from the First Class Judicial Magistrate or RDO of your district, then register at the ULB.
  • Apply at: CRS Tamil Nadu or nearest e-Sevai Centre.

Even after registration, people find themselves unable to download or print the electronic certificate due to prolonged server downtimes.

How to fix:

  • Wait 24–48 hours after registration approval and try downloading again from CRS Tamil Nadu.
  • Try a different browser (Google Chrome) and disable any VPN or ad blockers.
  • Visit the local Municipality/Town Panchayat office and request a printed, stamped physical copy.
  • If the issue persists for more than a week, contact: 1800-102-5553.

In cases where the death was not registered, obtaining a Non-Availability Certificate — needed to register the death later — can be a slow process.

How to fix:

  • Apply for the NAC at your local Municipality, Town Panchayat, or Gram Panchayat office in person.
  • Submit an affidavit stating reasons for non-registration along with supporting documents.
  • The NAC is typically issued within 7–10 working days after verification.
  • Use the NAC to proceed with late registration at the CRS Tamil Nadu Portal or e-Sevai Centre.

Inaccuracies in name spelling, incorrect age, or wrong date of death require going back to authorities for corrections, which can be slow.

How to fix:

  • Prepare a Notarised Affidavit clearly stating the error and the correct information.
  • Submit the affidavit along with the original certificate and supporting documents (hospital record, Aadhaar) to the issuing ULB office.
  • The correction is processed by the Health Officer / Registrar and a corrected certificate issued.
  • Track the status at CRS Tamil Nadu or e-Sevai Centre. Helpline: 1800-102-5553.
